title: Anti-Flood Device Activated
shortDescription: The anti-flood safety system has detected water in the base of the dishwasher.
fullDescription: Error AL01 indicates the float switch in the base tray has been activated, meaning water has leaked into the bottom of the dishwasher. The machine immediately stops all water intake and activates the drain pump to prevent flooding. This is a safety protection feature.

causes.cause: Leaking door seal allowing water into the base
Cracked or leaking wash pump housing
Loose or damaged internal hose connection
Leaking water inlet valve

symptoms: AL01 error displayed or 1 LED flash
Drain pump runs continuously
Machine will not start a wash cycle
Water visible underneath the dishwasher
Musty smell from base area

fixSteps.title: Disconnect and tilt the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and turn off the water supply. Carefully tilt the machine backward about 45 degrees to drain water from the base tray. Have towels ready.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check the door gasket around the entire perimeter for tears, deformation, or detergent buildup that prevents a proper seal. Clean or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Check internal hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the side panels and inspect all internal hose connections, especially at the wash pump, drain pump, and inlet valve. Look for drips, loose clamps, or cracked hoses.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the wash pump and sump
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wash pump housing and sump area for cracks. Run water through the system manually to identify the exact leak source.

whenToCallPro: If the leak source is not obvious or the wash pump housing is cracked, professional repair is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher AL01 anti flood leak fix

faq.q: Can I reset AL01 without fixing the leak?
faq.a: You can clear the error by draining the base tray and tilting the machine, but the error will return if the leak is not repaired.

faq.q: Is the anti-flood device a float switch?
faq.a: Yes, it is a polystyrene float in the base tray that rises when water accumulates, triggering a microswitch that signals the control board.

faq.q: Can too much detergent cause AL01?
faq.a: Yes, excessive suds can overflow through seals. Always use the recommended amount of dishwasher-specific detergent.

title: Water Intake Fault
shortDescription: The dishwasher is not receiving water or is not filling within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error AL02 indicates the dishwasher has not filled to the required water level within the timeout period. The control board detected that the pressure switch or flow meter did not register sufficient water intake. This is usually a water supply issue.

causes.cause: Water supply tap is turned off
Inlet hose is kinked or blocked
Inlet valve filter screen is clogged
Water inlet valve solenoid has failed

symptoms: AL02 error displayed or 2 LED flashes
No water enters the dishwasher
Machine stops early in the cycle
Humming sound from inlet valve area
Cycle does not progress past fill phase

fixSteps.title: Check the water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the water supply tap is fully open. Turn it off and on to confirm water flow. Check if other appliances in the house have water.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the inlet hose from the tap to the machine for kinks. Disconnect the hose from the machine and check the filter screen in the valve inlet. Clean any debris.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the machine attempting to fill, check for 230V at the inlet valve connector. If voltage is present but no water flows, the valve is stuck closed and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply. Disconnect the inlet hose. Remove the valve mounting screws and electrical connector. Install the new valve and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: Only if the inlet valve needs replacement and you are not comfortable with plumbing and electrical connections.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher AL02 water intake fill error fix

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause AL02?
faq.a: Yes, if water pressure is very low, the dishwasher may not fill within the timeout. Check pressure at other taps.

faq.q: What is an Aquastop hose?
faq.a: An Aquastop hose has a built-in shut-off valve that closes if the hose bursts or leaks. If the Aquastop has been triggered, the hose needs replacing.

faq.q: How do I clean the inlet filter?
faq.a: Disconnect the hose from the back of the machine. The filter screen is visible inside the valve inlet. Pull it out with needle-nose pliers and rinse under running water.

title: Drain Timeout Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher could not drain the water within the allowed time period.
fullDescription: Error AL03 indicates the drain cycle did not complete within the expected duration. Water remains in the sump after the drain pump has operated. This is typically caused by a blockage in the drain system or a failed drain pump.

causes.cause: Drain filter is clogged with food debris
Drain hose is kinked or blocked
Drain pump impeller is jammed
Drain pump motor has failed

symptoms: AL03 error displayed or 3 LED flashes
Standing water in the bottom of the dishwasher
Dishes are dirty with food residue
Unpleasant odor from standing water
Drain pump sounds strained or does not run

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm and filter assembly from the bottom of the dishwasher. Clean all components thoroughly under running water. Remove any food debris, glass, or other obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose from the dishwasher to the sink drain or waste pipe. Check for kinks, clogs, or improper routing. The hose should loop up to at least the height of the worktop to prevent siphoning.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter housing and check the drain pump impeller. Turn it by hand to ensure it moves freely. Remove any debris such as broken glass or fruit pips.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the impeller is free but the pump does not run, test for voltage at the pump connector during a drain cycle. If voltage is present but pump does not run, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump has failed and you are not comfortable replacing it.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher AL03 drain blockage error fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: After every 3-5 cycles for the coarse filter, and weekly for the fine mesh filter. Always scrape plates before loading.

faq.q: Can the sink drain cause AL03?
faq.a: Yes, a blocked sink waste pipe or garbage disposal can prevent the dishwasher from draining. Run hot water through the sink drain first.

faq.q: Why does the drain hose need a high loop?
faq.a: The high loop prevents dirty water from siphoning back into the dishwasher. The hose should rise to at least worktop height before going down to the drain.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor is providing an out-of-range reading or is disconnected.
fullDescription: Error AL04 indicates the NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient) thermistor used to measure wash water temperature has failed or is reading outside its expected range. Without accurate temperature data, the dishwasher cannot properly control the wash and rinse temperatures.

causes.cause: NTC sensor has failed (open or short circuit)
NTC wiring connector is loose or corroded
NTC wiring harness is damaged
Control board NTC input circuit fault

symptoms: AL04 error displayed or 4 LED flashes
Water does not heat during wash cycle
Dishes come out greasy or not clean
Cycle runs much longer than normal
Steam visible when it should not be (overheating)

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher and unplug for 10 minutes. Reconnect and run a cycle. Temperature glitches can sometimes clear with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Locate and test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The NTC is typically mounted in the sump or on the wash pump housing. Disconnect the sensor and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature expect approximately 10K-50K ohms depending on model.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring from the NTC to the control board. Look for damaged insulation, loose pins, or corrosion at connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor reads open or shorted, replace it. Pull the old sensor from its housing, insert the new one, and reconnect the wiring.

whenToCallPro: Only if the NTC and wiring test fine, suggesting a control board input circuit fault.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher AL04 temperature sensor NTC fix

faq.q: What temperature should the dishwasher reach?
faq.a: Normal wash cycles reach 55-65°C, intensive cycles up to 70°C, and eco cycles around 45-50°C.

faq.q: Can a faulty NTC damage dishes?
faq.a: If the NTC causes overheating, delicate items like plastics or glassware could be damaged. If it causes no heating, dishes will not be cleaned properly.

faq.q: Is the dishwasher NTC the same as a washer NTC?
faq.a: They use the same principle but may have different resistance values and mounting styles. Always order the correct part for your specific model.

title: Circulation Pump / Pressure Switch Fault
shortDescription: The wash circulation pump or the water level pressure switch is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error AL05 indicates either the wash circulation pump is not running or the pressure switch is not detecting the correct water level. The circulation pump is what drives water through the spray arms. Without proper water circulation, dishes cannot be cleaned.

causes.cause: Circulation pump motor has seized or failed
Circulation pump impeller is jammed by debris
Pressure switch hose is blocked or disconnected
Circulation pump capacitor has failed

symptoms: AL05 error displayed or 5 LED flashes
No spray arm movement during wash
Dishes come out dirty
Humming sound but no water circulation
Machine fills and drains but does not wash

fixSteps.title: Check for spray arm obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the spray arms and check they spin freely. Clear any food debris from the spray arm holes and the filter assembly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the circulation pump
fixSteps.instruction: Access the circulation pump from underneath the dishwasher. Check for debris jamming the impeller. Attempt to rotate the impeller by hand through the pump housing.

fixSteps.title: Test the pump electrically
fixSteps.instruction: Check the circulation pump motor for continuity (typically 3-15 ohms). If the pump has a start capacitor, test it as well.

fixSteps.title: Replace the circulation pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump motor has failed, disconnect the wiring and hose connections. Remove the mounting bracket and install the new pump assembly.

whenToCallPro: Circulation pump replacement involves significant disassembly. Professional repair is recommended unless you are experienced.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher AL05 circulation pump error fix

faq.q: What does the circulation pump do?
faq.a: The circulation pump forces water at high pressure through the spray arms to clean the dishes. Without it, dishes receive no water spray.

faq.q: Can a broken dish cause AL05?
faq.a: Yes, broken glass or china fragments can jam the circulation pump impeller, triggering AL05. Always check for broken items.

faq.q: Is the circulation pump the same as the drain pump?
faq.a: No, they are separate. The circulation pump is larger and drives water through the spray arms. The drain pump is smaller and removes waste water.

title: Water Inlet Valve Fault
shortDescription: The water inlet valve is not closing properly or is stuck open.
fullDescription: Error AL06 indicates the water inlet solenoid valve is malfunctioning. The valve may be stuck open causing overfilling, or the control board cannot control the valve properly. This differs from AL02 (no fill) as AL06 specifically indicates valve control issues.

causes.cause: Inlet valve solenoid is stuck open
Debris preventing valve from seating properly
Control board inlet valve triac has failed
Wiring to inlet valve is damaged

symptoms: AL06 error displayed or 6 LED flashes
Machine overfills with water
Water continues to flow after machine is stopped
Puddles of water on kitchen floor
Anti-flood protection may also activate

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: If the machine is overfilling, turn off the water supply tap right away. Then unplug the machine.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the inlet hose and inspect the valve seat for debris. Clean the filter screen. Sometimes grit prevents the valve from closing fully.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Measure the valve solenoid resistance (typically 2K-4K ohms). With no power applied, the valve should be fully closed. If it leaks water with no power, the diaphragm is stuck open.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply, disconnect inlet hose, remove valve mounting and electrical connector. Install new valve and reconnect everything.

whenToCallPro: If the valve itself is fine, the control board triac may be stuck on, requiring professional board repair or replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher AL06 inlet valve stuck open fix

faq.q: Can AL06 cause flooding?
faq.a: Yes, a stuck-open inlet valve can cause continuous water flow. Always turn off the water supply tap immediately when you see this error.

faq.q: Should I leave the water supply on when the dishwasher is not in use?
faq.a: It is good practice to turn off the water supply tap when the dishwasher is not running, especially on older machines, to prevent flooding from valve failures.

faq.q: What causes the valve to stick open?
faq.a: Mineral deposits, grit in the water supply, or wear of the rubber diaphragm inside the valve can prevent it from closing fully.

title: Motor / Alternating Wash Arm Fault
shortDescription: The wash motor or the alternating wash arm diverter is not working correctly.
fullDescription: Error AL07 indicates the motor that drives the spray arms or the diverter valve that alternates water between upper and lower spray arms has a fault. On some models this relates to the main wash pump motor; on others it is the diverter motor specifically.

causes.cause: Diverter valve motor has failed
Diverter valve is jammed by debris
Wash motor overheating and shutting down
Wiring to diverter or motor is damaged

symptoms: AL07 error displayed or 7 LED flashes
Only top or bottom rack cleaned properly
Clicking or grinding noise during wash
Spray arms do not rotate
Inconsistent wash results

fixSteps.title: Check spray arm rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and manually spin both spray arms. They should rotate freely without catching. Clear any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: The diverter is usually located at the bottom of the sump. Check for food debris or broken items jamming the mechanism. Clean thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Test the diverter motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the diverter motor and check for resistance (typically 200-800 ohms). Also verify it receives voltage from the control board during operation.

fixSteps.title: Replace the diverter assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the diverter motor has failed, replace the complete diverter assembly. Remove the sump cover, disconnect the old unit, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the main wash pump motor has failed, professional replacement is recommended due to the complexity of the repair.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher AL07 diverter motor spray arm fix

faq.q: What is a diverter valve?
faq.a: The diverter valve alternates water flow between the upper and lower spray arms during the wash cycle, ensuring both racks receive equal cleaning.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher without the diverter?
faq.a: The machine may run but only one set of spray arms will receive water, resulting in poor cleaning on one rack.

faq.q: Why are only top rack dishes clean?
faq.a: The diverter may be stuck in the upper position, directing all water to the top spray arm. Inspecting and cleaning the diverter should resolve this.

title: Heating Element Fault
shortDescription: The wash water heating element is not working or has a short circuit.
fullDescription: Error AL08 indicates the heating element used to heat the wash water has failed. The element may be open circuit (broken), short-circuited to ground, or the control board cannot supply power to it. Hot water is essential for effective dishwashing and sanitization.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out (open circuit)
Heating element shorted to ground (earth leakage)
Heater wiring connection is loose or corroded
Control board heater relay has failed

symptoms: AL08 error displayed or 8 LED flashes
Water stays cold during wash cycle
Dishes are greasy and poorly cleaned
Cycle runs for excessively long time
RCD/GFCI circuit breaker trips

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry a cycle. If AL08 returns, the heater circuit needs testing.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heater from underneath the machine. Disconnect the terminals and measure resistance (typically 15-30 ohms). Also check insulation to ground (must exceed 2 megaohms).

fixSteps.title: Check heater connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the heater terminal connections for corrosion, loose spade connectors, or heat damage. Clean and tighten as needed.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: If the heater has failed, drain remaining water, disconnect the old element, remove mounting hardware, and install the replacement. Some models have the heater integrated with the sump.

whenToCallPro: If the heater is leaking to earth and tripping the RCD, professional repair ensures safe reconnection.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher AL08 heating element replacement

faq.q: Can I wash with cold water?
faq.a: The dishwasher will attempt to run but cleaning quality will be very poor. Grease and detergent tablets require hot water to dissolve and clean effectively.

faq.q: Why does my RCD trip with AL08?
faq.a: A heater that is leaking electricity to ground (earth) will trip the RCD/GFCI. This is a safety fault that must be repaired before using the machine.

faq.q: Is the heater integrated into the sump?
faq.a: On some newer Ariston models, yes. This means the entire sump assembly may need to be replaced, which is more expensive.

title: EEPROM / Software Error
shortDescription: The EEPROM memory on the control board has a read/write error.
fullDescription: Error AL09 indicates the EEPROM chip on the main control board has a data corruption or communication error. This chip stores the cycle programs and machine configuration. When data is corrupted, the machine cannot execute wash programs correctly.

causes.cause: EEPROM data corruption from power interruption
Control board hardware fault
Power surge damage to EEPROM circuit
Board connector loose between modules

symptoms: AL09 error displayed or 9 LED flashes
Machine will not start any program
Incorrect cycle behavior
Display shows wrong information
Machine resets repeatedly

fixSteps.title: Extended power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher for 30 minutes to perform a full reset. Sometimes EEPROM data can recover after a complete power discharge.

fixSteps.title: Check board connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Open the machine and check all wire connectors on the control board. Reseat each one firmly. A loose connection can cause intermittent communication errors.

fixSteps.title: EEPROM reprogramming
fixSteps.instruction: An Ariston service technician may be able to reprogram the EEPROM using a diagnostic programmer and the correct firmware file for your model.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If reprogramming fails, replace the main control board with a new pre-programmed unit for your specific model.

whenToCallPro: EEPROM faults almost always require professional intervention for reprogramming or board replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher AL09 EEPROM control board error fix

faq.q: What causes EEPROM corruption?
faq.a: Power interruptions during a write cycle, voltage spikes, and aging can all corrupt EEPROM data. Using a surge protector helps prevent this.

faq.q: Can I buy a replacement board online?
faq.a: Yes, but ensure you order the exact part number for your model. The board must be pre-programmed with the correct firmware.

faq.q: Is it worth fixing AL09?
faq.a: If the machine is less than 8-10 years old, a control board replacement at 70-160 is generally worthwhile compared to a new dishwasher.

title: Heating Element Circuit / Relay Error
shortDescription: The heater relay or triac on the control board is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error AL10 indicates the control board cannot properly manage the heating element circuit. The relay or triac that switches the heater on and off may be stuck or damaged. This is a control board level fault that prevents safe temperature regulation.

causes.cause: Heater relay on control board welded or open
Heater element causing excessive current draw
Control board PCB trace damage
Power surge damaged relay circuit

symptoms: AL10 error displayed or 10 LED flashes
Water overheats during wash
No heating at all
Machine stops mid-cycle
Burning smell from control board area

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry. If AL10 returns, the control board needs inspection.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Check the heater resistance and insulation to ground. A shorted heater can damage the relay. If the heater is faulty, replace it.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burnt relay, discolored PCB, or melted components around the heater circuit area of the board.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the relay is damaged, replace the entire control board. Replace the heater as well if it tested faulty to prevent recurring damage.

whenToCallPro: AL10 involves the heater safety circuit. Professional repair is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher AL10 heater relay control board fix

faq.q: Is AL10 the same as AL08?
faq.a: No. AL08 means the heater itself has failed. AL10 means the control board circuit that manages the heater has failed. Both result in temperature problems but have different causes.

faq.q: Can I replace just the relay?
faq.a: Technically yes with skilled soldering, but full board replacement is more reliable and the standard repair approach.

faq.q: What causes relay failure?
faq.a: Aging, heater short circuits causing current surges, and power spikes can all damage the relay contacts.

title: Anti-Flood Protection (Alternate Code)
shortDescription: Water detected in the base tray — this is the numeric variant of the AL01 anti-flood alarm.
fullDescription: Error A01 is the numeric variant of AL01, displayed on newer Ariston dishwasher models. It indicates the same anti-flood safety activation where water has been detected in the base tray. The cause, diagnosis, and repair are identical to AL01.

causes.cause: Door gasket leak allowing water into the base
Internal hose connection has come loose
Wash pump seal is leaking
Cracked sump or tub component

symptoms: A01 error displayed on newer models
Drain pump runs continuously even when turned off
Water pooling under the dishwasher
Machine will not start or stops immediately
Musty or damp smell

fixSteps.title: Tilt and drain the base
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine. Carefully tilt it back about 45 degrees to drain accumulated water from the base tray. Have towels and a basin ready to catch water.

fixSteps.title: Identify the leak source
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the side panels and look for water trails or drip marks on internal components. Run a short fill cycle while watching for the leak source.

fixSteps.title: Check door seal and hose clamps
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door gasket for tears or deformation. Check all internal hose clamps are tight. Pay special attention to the connection between the sump and the wash pump.

fixSteps.title: Repair or replace leaking component
fixSteps.instruction: Once the leak source is identified, replace the gasket, tighten the clamp, or replace the component as needed.

whenToCallPro: If the leak is from the wash pump seal or sump, professional disassembly and repair is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher A01 anti flood leak repair

faq.q: Is A01 the same as AL01?
faq.a: Yes, they are the same error. Newer Ariston models display it as A01 while older models show AL01.

faq.q: How does the anti-flood system work?
faq.a: A polystyrene float sits in the base tray. When water leaks in, the float rises and activates a microswitch that tells the control board to stop water intake and run the drain pump.

faq.q: Can I just dry out the base tray?
faq.a: Tilting and drying clears the error temporarily, but the leak will cause it to recur. You must find and fix the source of the leak.

title: Drain Fault (Numeric Code)
shortDescription: The dishwasher failed to drain within the expected time — numeric variant of AL03.
fullDescription: Error A03 is the newer numeric format of AL03. It indicates the dishwasher could not drain water within the timeout period. The drain system is blocked, the pump has failed, or the drain hose is obstructed. Diagnosis and repair are the same as AL03.

causes.cause: Food debris clogging the drain filter
Drain hose blocked or kinked
Drain pump impeller jammed
Drain pump motor burned out

symptoms: A03 error displayed on newer models
Water standing in the bottom of the tub
Bad smell from stagnant water
Dishes left dirty after failed cycle
Gurgling sound from drain but no drainage

fixSteps.title: Clean filters thoroughly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean all filter components from the dishwasher floor. The coarse filter, fine mesh filter, and micro filter should all be removed and cleaned under running water.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filters removed, look into the pump well. Remove the cover if present and check the impeller for obstructions like glass shards, fruit pips, or toothpicks.

fixSteps.title: Inspect drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the drain hose for kinks and blockages. Disconnect from the sink/waste and blow through it to confirm it is clear. Ensure proper high loop installation.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump impeller is free but the pump does not run, test electrically and replace if failed.

whenToCallPro: If filters and drain path are clear but the pump has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher A03 drain error fix

faq.q: Is A03 the same as AL03?
faq.a: Yes, A03 is the newer numeric code for the same drain fault. Older models display AL03.

faq.q: Can I manually drain the dishwasher?
faq.a: Yes, use towels or a sponge to soak up water from the bottom. You can also use the emergency drain hose if your model has one.

faq.q: Should I scrape dishes before loading?
faq.a: Yes, removing large food particles before loading significantly reduces drain blockages and improves cleaning performance.

title: Wash Motor / Diverter Fault (Numeric Code)
shortDescription: The wash motor or spray arm diverter has a fault — numeric variant of AL07.
fullDescription: Error A07 is the newer format of AL07, indicating the wash motor or diverter valve is not functioning correctly. The spray arms may not be receiving water or the motor is not driving them properly. This results in poor or no cleaning.

causes.cause: Diverter valve motor stuck or failed
Debris blocking spray arm movement
Wash motor thermal overload tripped
Control board diverter output failed

symptoms: A07 error displayed on newer models
Dishes not cleaned properly on one or both racks
No spray arm rotation visible
Grinding or clicking noise
Water sitting in sump but not circulating

fixSteps.title: Check spray arms and filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the spray arms. Ensure all spray holes are clear. Clean the filter assembly. Check that nothing is blocking spray arm rotation.

fixSteps.title: Allow motor to cool
fixSteps.instruction: If the wash motor overheated, let the machine cool for 30 minutes. The thermal overload may reset automatically.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the diverter
fixSteps.instruction: Access the diverter mechanism and check for debris. Test the diverter motor resistance and check for voltage during operation.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the diverter motor or wash pump motor as needed.

whenToCallPro: If the main wash pump has failed, professional replacement is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher A07 wash motor diverter fix

faq.q: Is A07 the same as AL07?
faq.a: Yes, newer models display A07 instead of AL07 for the same fault.

faq.q: Can overloading cause A07?
faq.a: Yes, items blocking the spray arms or overloading that restricts water flow can cause the wash motor to overload and trigger A07.

faq.q: What is the difference between the wash pump and drain pump?
faq.a: The wash pump is larger and forces water through the spray arms at high pressure. The drain pump is smaller and removes waste water at the end of cycles.

title: Communication Error Between Boards
shortDescription: The display module and main control board cannot communicate with each other.
fullDescription: Error A12 indicates a communication breakdown between the user interface module (display board) and the main electronic control board. This is typically caused by a loose cable connection, faulty display board, or main board failure.

causes.cause: Interconnect cable between boards is loose
Display board has failed
Main control board communication port failed
Moisture damage to board connectors

symptoms: A12 error displayed
Display freezes or goes blank
Buttons unresponsive
Machine does not respond to commands
Random symbols on display

fixSteps.title: Reseat cable connections
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door panel and reseat the cable connecting the display board to the main board. Ensure it clicks firmly into place.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect both boards and the connector for any signs of moisture, condensation, or corrosion. Dry and clean with electronics contact cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Test with power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: After reseating, power cycle the machine. If A12 persists, one of the boards likely needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the display board
fixSteps.instruction: Start with the less expensive display board. If that does not fix it, the main board needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If reseating does not work and you are unsure which board to replace.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher A12 communication error board fix

faq.q: Can steam cause A12?
faq.a: Yes, steam and moisture can corrode board connectors over time, causing intermittent communication faults.

faq.q: Which board should I replace first?
faq.a: Start with the interconnect cable (cheapest), then the display board. Only replace the main board as a last resort.

faq.q: How can I prevent A12?
faq.a: Ensure the dishwasher door seal is in good condition to prevent steam from reaching the electronics. Avoid opening the door mid-cycle when the interior is very hot.

title: Drying Fan or Condenser Fault
shortDescription: The drying system fan or condenser circuit is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: Error A14 indicates the drying system is malfunctioning. On models with active drying (fan-assisted or condenser drying), this means the fan motor or condenser circuit has failed. Dishes will be wet after the cycle completes.

causes.cause: Drying fan motor has failed
Fan blade is jammed or broken
Condenser system vent is blocked
Control board drying circuit failure

symptoms: A14 error displayed
Dishes and interior are wet after cycle
No fan sound during drying phase
Excessive condensation on dishes
Cycle completes but drying is ineffective

fixSteps.title: Check the vent path
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the drying vent (usually on the side or rear interior wall). Ensure it is not blocked by dishes or debris. Clean the vent opening.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drying fan
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drying fan from the side panel. Check that the fan blade spins freely and is not cracked or damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the fan motor and test resistance (typically 50-200 ohms). Also check for voltage at the connector during the drying phase.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the fan motor has failed, remove the mounting screws, disconnect wiring, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the fan and vent are fine but drying still fails, the control board drying circuit may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston dishwasher A14 drying fan error fix

faq.q: Do all Ariston dishwashers have active drying?
faq.a: No, budget models use residual heat drying (no fan). A14 only applies to models with a drying fan or condenser system.

faq.q: Why are my dishes wet even without an error code?
faq.a: Residual heat drying works by evaporation. Opening the door slightly at the end of the cycle and using rinse aid improves drying. This is normal, not a fault.

faq.q: Can I skip the drying cycle?
faq.a: Yes, you can open the door after the wash and rinse cycles to air dry. The A14 error only prevents automatic drying, not the wash function.
